message.content: {"items":[{"title":"German Reich (1871–1945): Elections to the Reichstag of the Weimar Republic 1919–1933","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8013","justification":"Official election results for Reichstag elections 1919–1933, covering the 1920s–1930s in the German Reich; key for studying political history and electoral change in the Weimar era."},{"title":"Demographic Data for the German Reich and the Federal Republic of Germany, 1840–1945 (Regional Data for Towns and Administrative Units)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8142","justification":"Historical demographic statistics for the German Reich up to 1945, including the interwar years; enables research on population history and social structure in the 1920s and 1930s."},{"title":"Historical Election Data for the German Reich 1871–1933","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8011","justification":"Long-run election series for Reichstag elections from the Empire through Weimar; contains detailed results for the 1920s–early 1930s across constituencies of the German Reich."},{"title":"Municipal Statistics in the German Reich 1925–1939","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8140","justification":"Time series of municipal-level statistics (population, economy, welfare, etc.) for 1925–1939 in the German Reich; suitable for local social and economic history in the interwar period."},{"title":"Public Employment in the German Reich 1925–1939","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8141","justification":"Historical data on public sector employment in the German Reich between 1925 and 1939; supports research on state structures, administration, and labor in the late Weimar and early Nazi period."},{"title":"German Socio-Economic Panel for the Weimar Republic and Nazi Period (Regional Indicators 1919–1939)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8331","justification":"Regional indicators (unemployment, wages, industrial structure, etc.) for the German Reich between 1919 and 1939; useful for studying economic and social history in the 1920s and 1930s."},{"title":"Occupational Structure and Social Stratification in the German Reich, 1882–1939","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8329","justification":"Historical occupational data for the German Reich up to 1939; allows analysis of class structure and social change in the 1920s and 1930s."},{"title":"Religious Denominations in the German Reich 1871–1939 (District-Level Data)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8330","justification":"District-level series on religious composition in the German Reich through 1939; relevant for cultural and religious history in the interwar decades."},{"title":"Historical Statistics of German Cities 1925–1939","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8143","justification":"City-level time series for the German Reich, 1925–1939, including demographic and economic indicators; valuable for urban history in the 1920s–1930s."},{"title":"Weimar and Nazi Germany: Aggregate Economic Indicators 1919–1939","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8332","justification":"Macro-level historical economic data for the German Reich between 1919 and 1939; supports research on the economic history of the Weimar Republic and early Nazi era."}]}
